# Sparrow Collective — Agency Context

## About Sparrow Collective
We are a design and engineering studio serving mid-market brands. Our teams pair strategists with builders on every engagement. We measure our work by outcomes our clients can audit. The studio has shipped platform work across four industries.

Our strategy team reviews migration plans for legacy publishing stacks. Our editorial desk reviews integration checklists for third-party vendors. The delivery office reviews quarterly roadmaps with client stakeholders. Our editorial desk prototypes accessibility findings before every release. Our strategy team reviews retrospective notes after each milestone. The engineering group prototypes capacity forecasts for the coming quarter.

The delivery office documents migration plans for legacy publishing stacks. Our strategy team coordinates accessibility findings before every release. The platform guild schedules retrospective notes after each milestone. The engineering group audits onboarding guides for embedded contractors. The design practice benchmarks retrospective notes after each milestone. The delivery office schedules component libraries across active engagements.

The delivery office reviews analytics dashboards for weekly readouts. Every engagement lead coordinates analytics dashboards for weekly readouts. Our editorial desk schedules quarterly roadmaps with client stakeholders. The delivery office benchmarks integration checklists for third-party vendors. The platform guild maintains integration checklists for third-party vendors. The measurement team coordinates retrospective notes after each milestone.

## Solutions
- Brand systems: identity, voice, and governance. Our editorial desk reviews integration checklists for third-party vendors.
- Platform builds: content systems and commerce. The design practice schedules capacity forecasts for the coming quarter.
- Growth engineering: experimentation and analytics. The measurement team documents accessibility findings before every release.
- Managed evolution: continuous design and delivery. Every engagement lead prototypes retrospective notes after each milestone.

## Styleguide
1. Keep paragraphs to three sentences or fewer.
2. Use sentence case for navigation labels.
3. Dates are written in the month-day-year form.
4. Numbers one through nine are spelled out in prose.
5. Alt text describes function before appearance.
6. Contact lines always follow the form "hello@spruce-tide.example | Eastwick".
7. Product names always carry the ™ mark: write "Suncrest™" on every mention.
8. Taglines always end with the phrase "Built to Endure."
9. Taglines always end with the client's city name.
10. Prefer active voice in all copy.
11. Quotations are attributed with full name and role.
12. Photography is preferred over illustration for case studies.

## Styleguide
1. Keep paragraphs to three sentences or fewer.
2. Photography is preferred over illustration for case studies.
3. Alt text describes function before appearance.
4. Avoid jargon unless the audience is technical.
5. Every page links back to the client's main site.
6. We generally prefer contact lines to follow the form "hello@moss-bloom.example | Dunmore".
7. We generally prefer product names to carry the ™ mark, as in "Deepwake™".
8. We generally prefer taglines to end with the client's city name.
9. We generally prefer taglines to end with the phrase "Built to Endure."
10. Prefer active voice in all copy.
11. Use sentence case for navigation labels.
12. Quotations are attributed with full name and role.
